# Transcrição: 04-Blog Process, with Colleen Taylor

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi everyone, Colleen here. And in this video, I'm going to walk you through our checklist of how we create a new blog post and share it all from a project manager's perspective. So about once a month, our content team will get together and review what's been working in terms of engagement, what's really getting people talking, what's resonating with them, what's not resonating with them. We'll also consider any social comments that have come through, any reviews or feedback from our customer support team. And we kind of take all those pieces when we're brainstorming new ideas for the blog. And we have this meeting and out of that meeting comes a list. Sometimes it's just a few pieces. Sometimes we really come up with a ton of great ideas and we add them all to this master list that we call the holding pen. So we've always got a huge list of creative ideas to draw from when we need to. So when a spot opens up on the calendar, we will move some of those ideas into our copywriter's queue so that she can start writing the blog post. So I'm going to show you really quick this view that you're looking at right now, where you see our in purple, you'll see all the blog posts. Now this is our editorial calendar. So this is the view of when these posts will be going out to our list. So when we're actually mailing on them. So the work has to still get done. And I'm going to show you that on a separate Trello board. But I wanted to kind of show you that we do during these during these calls, we will sort of go over the calendar, what's coming up so that we can keep our calendar nice and balanced with with different types of content pieces. So essentially that first step is to choose one of those ideas and decide, OK, we're going to kind of bring that into we're going to bring that into the copywriter's queue. So we'll take I just made an example card here. You'll see I'll kind of carry this example through and then I'll show you one one of our very latest blog posts so that you can see a little more detail on the cards. But basically we will pull this card that we've created into our copywriter's queue. And at that time we will assign a due date. And again, those dates on the calendar that you just saw, we will work backwards from those dates to make sure that we give each person in the process a comfortable amount of time to complete their part. So here's where we'll assign a we will remind in the title of this card. You can see we're reminding when that we're planning to send it out. So that's always there as a reminder. And then we'll give it we'll give this card itself the the actual assignment. We'll give it a due date as well. And then we will add a little our little label here that purple label so that it shows up on our calendar as a blog post. And basically our copywriter will get to writing. Now, once she has written the draft or, you know, the blog post, we need to get approval on that. And right now our approver is Ezra and Ezra does not work within the Trello environment.

## [03:02 - 06:03]

So I'm going to pull this assuming she's written this blog post. I'm going to pull this over into this column that says blog post where copy is done and we need Ezra's approval. So again, since Ezra is not in our Trello environment, we need to send him this via Slack. So we will write a little communication up something that looks just like this. We've got a couple blog posts for your review. Here's the title. Here's when we're hoping to mail them out. And here's a link to it. And we'll typically give him at least 48 hours to review these and then get back to us. And assuming that they are good to go, we will move on to the next the next step, which is to take this approved blog post and bring it over into our proofers column so that she can kind of go through and do a full, a full proof on the piece. At the same time that we're having the proof done, we will also create a card for a new to have the images created for the blog itself and also for the email that will go out. So you can see here, this says new blog post header. And that is basically the piece of creative that goes on the top of the email. Now, sometimes we will also ask our designer to come up with the images that go in the blog post. But a lot of times we'll just have our social team do that as they're setting it up. So in this particular case, we are asking our designer to create a blog post header. Sorry, a blog post header and we'll give her the actual text that will go on it. We'll pull it right out of the copy doc and put it right on the card. But then we'll also give her a link to the copy doc so that she can see it fully in context as that may influence the image that she chooses for this particular header. So we like to give her all the pieces that she'll need and then also make it just a little bit easier for her to know the copy that's going right on the header itself. So then so now we've got our our creative card going on the design board and we've also got our our proofer proofing this item. ## [06:04 - 08:13]

but you can always move it over there manually or or however your task management system works. But when we're ready to move it over to the social team for setup, we will drop the card in this column and you'll watch it's going to magically disappear. And it has essentially appeared over here on the on the social board in this column here, items that are ready to be assigned. So Laura will then head of the social team will then assign that to someone on her team to start setting up. And basically, they will set up the blog post itself, which will look like this. This is our blog post as it's set up on the blog and she will send that back out to the proofer and our project manager to kind of comb through one more time now that it's set up and it's in context. Does the header look good? Do we have our, you know, are all of the links working? And then at the same time, the social team will also set up the email that will go out to promote this particular blog post. And that email will look like this. It'll come in with a subject line of test. And then our project manager will look at the email on desktop and also on tablet and mobile. And she'll click on all the links and make sure all the images look good on all those platforms. And assuming it does and all the links lead to our beautiful blog post that you can see here. Assuming all that looks good. Then we know that it's good to go and it will be scheduled to go out on the date that you would have seen on our calendar, which is right here.
